name: Bloch theorem for periodic potentials
principles: single-particle Schrödinger equation in a periodic potential
conventions: V(r) periodic with the Bravais lattice: V(r + R) = V(r) for any lattice vector R
n is the band index, k is the crystal momentum (Bloch wavevector) restricted to the first Brillouin zone
u_nk(r) has the periodicity of the lattice
valid for non-interacting electrons in a static periodic potential — the foundation of single-particle band theory
expectedLimits: k = 0 (Gamma point): psi reduces to u_n0(r), the lattice-periodic ground band edge wavefunction
k -> k + G (reciprocal lattice vector): same physical state, so band structure is periodic in reciprocal space
free-electron limit (V -> 0): psi -> e^{i k r}, plane wave; bands fold at zone boundaries with vanishing gaps
tight-binding limit (deep wells): u_nk localised on atoms, dispersion proportional to hopping integrals
irreducible Brillouin zone: symmetry reduces the unique k-space volume by the order of the point group
references: Bloch (1929) — Über die Quantenmechanik der Elektronen in Kristallgittern
Ashcroft & Mermin — Solid State Physics, ch.8
Kittel — Introduction to Solid State Physics, ch.7
Martin — Electronic Structure: Basic Theory and Practical Methods, ch.4
